name: osoi5-feopt2-w20-e1-lmhead12k-fa2sw-precache-strict-byteexact-splitkv399-v1
description: Strict byte-exact FIXED-ORDER split-KV rung (lawine #496, 42qroec1: 399.75 local TPS, +42.1 over the surgical-357 2D byte-exact rung, PPL 2.3767, 128/128, 0/8 kernel flips). Byte-identical to fa2sw_precache_kenyan EXCEPT BYTEEXACT_FIXED_TPS=4 + BYTEEXACT_NUM_SEGMENTS=64 pin vLLM's 3D split-KV tiles_per_segment to a fixed constant, so each parallel softmax segment covers a FIXED ABSOLUTE key span [s*64, (s+1)*64) regardless of seq_len (T=4 tiles x TILE_SIZE=16 = 64 keys/segment; 64 segments cover 4096 = max_model_len). The reduction order at any absolute key position is then M-invariant, so the M=8 spec-verify is byte-identical to the M=1 AR decode of the same token (Thinking-Machines 'fix the split SIZE not the split COUNT'). This recovers ~92% of the deployed-minus-surgical split-KV parallelism the surgical 2D path gives up, while staying byte-exact. Composes with splitkv-verify (which routes the M=8 verify onto this same 3D path). Crucially does NOT set VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT=1, so init_batch_invariance() never installs the ~48% matmul tax -- MLP/QKV/lm_head keep the fast Marlin path. SPECULATIVE_CONFIG kept (spec-alive MTP drafter, K_spec 7). int4 quant / precache / lm_head-prune / fa-sliding all unchanged. Armed on the STOCK wheel by re-jitting kernel_unified_attention + reduce_segments via sitecustomize -> byteexact_splitkv_patch (no installed-wheel file edit); off by default -> served compute path byte-identical to parent. Spec-alive -> NOT private-safe (penalize-lane candidate); the floor-lock 161.70 remains the only private-safe ship.
